summary: Partially matched Link's neutral special implementation by refactoring ftLk_SpecialN code shape, local constants, stack padding, and typed fighter object usage. The decomp.dev report recorded 4 new full function matches in ftLk_SpecialN and 2 additional unmatched-function improvements, raising matched code by 1052 bytes (+0.03%).
what_changed: Refactored most ftLk_SpecialN prototypes and local variables from Link-specific typedef aliases ftLk_GObj and ftLk_Fighter to Fighter_GObj and Fighter.
Removed ftLk_Fighter and ftLk_GObj typedef aliases from ftLink/forward.h, and propagated that cleanup into ftLk_SpecialN.h and ftCl_Init.h.

Reworked ftLk_SpecialN animation helpers: renamed anonymous inline helpers from foo/bar/baz to inlineA0/inlineA1/inlineA2/inlineA3, factored repeated bow/hand position logic into inlineA2, and reused it in start/loop air and ground animations.

Modeled the previous ftLk_Init_Vec3Group rodata differently: removed the zero-initialized ftLk_Init_Vec3Group definition from ftLk_Init.c, moved the relevant rodata split from ftLk_Init.c to ftLk_SpecialN.c, and represented the data in symbols.txt as local @334/@335/@336 Vec3-sized objects.
Changed sdata2 symbol scopes for ftLk_Init_804D9340 and ftLk_Init_804D9344 from global to local and renamed the 0x804D9348 float to local @290.
Added placeholder.h and trigf.h includes in ftLk_SpecialN.c, removed lb/lbrefract.h, and used FORCE_PAD_STACK/PAD_STACK macros in places that previously used dummy u8 stack arrays.

Adjusted several numeric literals to float-typed values in motion-state calls and assignments, such as 0.0f and 1.0f, and added a documented float-order hack `(void) (5 * deg_to_rad);` in ftLk_SpecialN_Enter.

Corrected or reshaped behavior in matching-sensitive spots, including ftLk_SpecialAirNLoop_IASA using PAD_STACK(8) and ftLk_MS_SpecialAirNEnd, and ftLk_SpecialAirNEnd_Coll calling ftCommon_8007D7FC in the air transition path.
Removed an unused padding tail from struct ftLk_FighterVars in ftLink/types.h.
smart_moves: The PR did not try to fully rename or understand every unknown helper; instead it used conservative inlineA* helper names while preserving code-shape control for matching.
Replacing fake stack arrays with FORCE_PAD_STACK and PAD_STACK made the matching intent explicit and reusable without pretending the arrays are meaningful game data.
Splitting the old 0x24 Vec3Group rodata into three local Vec3 constants aligned the config and source layout with how the compiler emitted local zero vectors for ftLk_SpecialN.
Moving away from ftLk_Fighter/ftLk_GObj aliases reduced type indirection and aligned Link/Young Link code with shared fighter conventions.
Factoring repeated hand/root position sampling into inlineA2 improved multiple animation functions at once and produced full matches for both ground and air loop animation functions.
The float-order hack was explicitly documented as a matching tactic, which helps future cleanup distinguish intentional codegen steering from normal logic.
decomp_lessons: For fighter files, prefer shared Fighter and Fighter_GObj types over character-specific typedef aliases unless there is evidence for a real distinct type.
When a zero-initialized aggregate does not match as a single global object, try modeling it as separate local const objects in the owning translation unit and update splits/symbols accordingly.
Repeated animation-body code may need to become a static inline helper rather than a normal function or macro to reproduce compiler output across several action-state callbacks.
Use explicit float literals in Fighter_ChangeMotionState and related calls when integer literals perturb codegen or constant ordering.
Stack-padding macros are preferable to unexplained dummy byte arrays once the purpose is only matching stack layout.
Partial matches can still justify header and symbol cleanup when the cleanup is tied to codegen, rodata ownership, or decomp.dev-confirmed progress.
naming_conventions: Use Fighter* for fighter user data and Fighter_GObj* for fighter object parameters instead of ftLk_Fighter* and ftLk_GObj* aliases.
Keep action callback suffixes consistent: _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll for ftLk_SpecialNStart, ftLk_SpecialNLoop, ftLk_SpecialNEnd, and their air variants.
Command variable enum entries use a cmd_ prefix, as in cmd_unk0_bool, cmd_unk1_bool, cmd_unk2, and cmd_unk3.
Temporary matching helpers may remain weakly named, such as inlineA0/inlineA1/inlineA2/inlineA3, when the PR has no stronger semantic evidence.
Local anonymous constants in symbols.txt may use @NNN labels when evidence does not support a stable named global symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used FORCE_PAD_STACK_4, FORCE_PAD_STACK_8, FORCE_PAD_STACK_16, and PAD_STACK(8) to influence stack layout.
Added placeholder.h, apparently to make stack-padding or placeholder macros available.
Inserted `(void) (5 * deg_to_rad);` with a `Float order hack` TODO to force constant ordering.
Changed integer constants to float literals, including 0.0f and 1.0f, in motion-state transitions.
Converted a single ftLk_Init_Vec3Group aggregate into local zero_a, zero_b, and zero_c Vec3 constants used to initialize local Vec3 variables before lb_8000B1CC calls.
Made MotionFlags constants static const, including `mf = Ft_MF_SkipModel | Ft_MF_SkipItemVis` and collision flags for transition helpers.
Updated GALE01 splits so rodata 0x803B7520-0x803B7544 belongs to ftLk_SpecialN.c instead of ftLk_Init.c.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 39.04%, +0.03%, +1052 bytes.
The decomp.dev bot reported 4 new full matches: ftLk_SpecialAirNLoop_Anim (+40 bytes), ftLk_SpecialNLoop_Anim (+32 bytes), ftLk_SpecialAirNLoop_IASA (+1 byte), and ftLk_SpecialAirNEnd_Coll (+1 byte).

The decomp.dev bot reported 2 unmatched-function improvements: ftLk_SpecialAirNStart_Anim improved from 83.69% to 94.85% (+91 bytes), and ftLk_SpecialNStart_Anim improved from 92.93% to 95.31% (+19 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftLk_SpecialNStart_Anim or ftLk_SpecialAirNStart_Anim.
Search for other fighter files still using character-specific aliases like ftLk_GObj or ftLk_Fighter.
Audit whether ftLk_Init_Vec3Group declaration in ftLk_SpecialN.h was later removed or renamed after local rodata modeling.
Look for later cleanup of inlineA0/inlineA1/inlineA2/inlineA3 names once semantics became clearer.
Search for other PRs using FORCE_PAD_STACK and float-order hacks in fighter special move matching.
